title: Pungent Creamy Russian Buckwheat Pilaf
description: A savory Russian dish featuring buckwheat, crispy fried chicken, and a creamy sauce, perfect for a midnight craving. This sugar-free friendly recipe is a great option for a satisfying meal.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken to the skillet and cook until browned and crispy, about 5 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional minute.
Add the buckwheat to the skillet and cook, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes.
Add the smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Add the cooked chicken back to the skillet and stir to combine with the buckwheat mixture.
Reduce heat to low and stir in the sour cream.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Stir in the chopped fresh dill and serve hot.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
